_comment: M9-T5 (+M9-T6's fixture 07): per-fixture max allowed % of differing pixels (PliegoOracle\PixelDiff, /out/NN-diff.png). Calibrated HONESTLY against a real local run (node+Chromium+Ghostscript all available) -- see tools/oracle's task-5-report.md (01-06) and task-6-report.md (07) for the measured %, per-fixture root cause (font ascent/leading metric nuances, table auto/fixed column-width algorithm, border-collapse approximation, and for 07: Bootstrap's fluid calc(rem + vw) heading font-sizes falling back to the plain UA h1/h2/h3 default -- vw has no meaning in a print engine, see that fixture's own <style> and the M9 README section for the full writeup) and the exact margin applied on top of each measured value. NOT arbitrary round numbers.
_04_comment: M10-T2: 2.0 -> 0.5 (TIGHTENED, never loosened without cause -- the reverse direction is always fine). Measured dropped 1.471% -> 0.089% (16.5x) as a SIDE EFFECT of this task's flex-item fix (Box\BoxTreeBuilder::collectChildren()/wrapAnonymousFlexItems(), see the task report): fixture 04 is flex-layout.html, whose flex rows mix real text/inline-element content the old code merged incorrectly (two adjacent Display::Inline children of a flex container used to collapse into ONE shared item instead of two, see the fix's own docblock) -- now genuinely fixed for real Bootstrap markup too (it is what motivated the fix, found investigating fixture 07's navbar). 0.5 keeps a ~5.6x margin over the new measured value, the same generous-margin-for-a-well-behaved-fixture shape as 05/06 (0.144/0.151 measured against a 1.0 threshold, ~6.6-6.9x) rather than a tight ratio -- 04 has no remaining KNOWN root cause the way 07 still does, so there is no reason to keep it artificially close to the old ceiling.
_07_comment: M10-T2: 5.5 -> 3.5 (TIGHTENED). History: 4.665% (pre-M10-T1) -> 5.654% (post-M10-T1's vw/vh fix, correctness win that unmasked a pre-existing navbar height bug, NOT recalibrated per that task's own instructions, see m10-task-1-report.md's 'Corrected analysis') -> 5.558% (this task's media-query fix alone, PART 1 -- min-width:576/768 and max-width:991.98/1199.98/1399.98 now genuinely apply against fixture 07's 793.70px A4 width, e.g. Bootstrap's .row-cols-md-3 grid; small win, the navbar gap was still the dominant mass) -> 5.523% (this task's navbar fix, PART 2 -- see below, barely moved fixture 07 specifically because a SECOND, larger issue below the navbar dominated the diff mass by then) -> 2.641% (this task's line-height inheritance fix, found investigating why the navbar fix barely moved 07's number -- see below). Root causes fixed this task, in the order their diff mass was found: (1) PART 1, media queries (Css\MediaQueryEvaluator, this task); (2) PART 2 navbar investigation: Box\BoxTreeBuilder::collectChildren()/wrapAnonymousFlexItems() merged TWO adjacent Display::Inline flex-item children (.navbar-brand `<a>`, .navbar-text `<span>`, both with real Bootstrap padding) into ONE shared anonymous flex item instead of two separate ones (css-flexbox-1 §4 violation) -- pliego's navbar rendered 40px tall against Chrome's real 56px; fixed, hand-verified now IDENTICAL (56px both, tools/oracle/probe-navbar.mjs); (3) found while explaining why (2) barely moved 07's own number despite being a real, verified fix: Style\ComputedStyle's line-height inheritance carried the ALREADY-RESOLVED px value straight down the tree instead of re-deriving a bare-NUMBER line-height (css-inline-3 §5.2, Bootstrap's `body{line-height:1.5}`) against each descendant's OWN font-size -- `.small`/`.card-text` (14px) inherited body's 24px (16*1.5) unchanged instead of the correct 21px (14*1.5), a ~3px-per-line drift compounding across every line below the card row. Fixed (Style\ComputedStyle::$lineHeightMultiplier). 3.5 keeps a comfortable ~1.3x margin over the new 2.641% measured value -- deliberately NOT as generous a ratio as 01-06/04 (which have no remaining open root cause): fixture 07 is the largest, most varied real-Bootstrap-markup fixture in this oracle and has a track record of surfacing new root causes when other things change nearby (see the whole history above), so its margin stays tighter/more conservative by design, consistent with the ratio it has always been calibrated against historically (~1.18x at its very first M9-T6 calibration).
